Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»SA Men’s 4x100m relay team qualifies for Paris Olympics

South Africa’s men’s relay teams shine at World Relay Event, securing silver and qualifying for Paris Olympics in stunning performances.

South Africa’s men’s relay teams delivered outstanding performances at theWorld Relay Event in the Bahamas this weekend. The men’s 4×400 m relay team not only secured a spot at the Paris Olympics but also clinched a silver medal in the finals on Monday morning (SA time).

Despite the absence ofWayde van Niekerk, the quartet of Gardeo Isaacs, Zakithi Nene, Matt Nortjé, and Lythe Pillay powered their way to a second-place finish in 3:00.75.

Botswana’s team, consisting of Busang Collen Kebinatshipi, Letsile Tebogo, Leungo Scotch, and Bayapo Ndori, claimed gold in 2:59.11, setting the fastest time this year. Belgium’s team secured the bronze medal with a time of 3:01.16.

The South African men’s 4×100 m relay team faced a setback during the first qualifying round, failing to secure a spot for the Paris Olympics. However, they bounced back in the second qualifying round, with Bayanda Walaza, Benjamin Richardson, Bradley Nkoana, and Akani Simbine winning their qualifier in an impressive time of 38.08 seconds.

This performance not only earned them a place in the Paris Olympics but also recorded the fastest overall time in the second qualifying round.

Although the men’s 4×100 m relay team did not advance to the finals in Nassau, their time in the second qualifying round would have been good enough for a bronze medal in the finals.

The American team, consisting of Courtney Lindsey, Kenneth Bednarek, Kyree King, and Noah Lyles, dominated the finals with a world-leading time of 37.40. Canada (37.89) and France (38.44) claimed silver and bronze, respectively.

The South African mixed relay team in the 4×400 m event, comprising Mthi Mthimkulu, Shirley Nekhubui, Lythe Pillay, and Zenéy Geldenhuys, finished third in the second qualifying round. Unfortunately, with only the top two countries automatically qualifying for the Paris Games, they narrowly missed out on a spot.

However, they still have a chance to qualify based on the world rankings at the end of the qualifying period between 31 December 2022 and 30 June 2024.South African record holder over 200 m, Clarence Munyai, had a challenging day, which ultimately affected the team’s performance in the first round. The decision to replace him with Bradley Nkoana in the second round proved to be a strategic success.

As the qualifying period for the Paris Olympics continues, the South African relay teams will undoubtedly be motivated by their strong performances at the World Relay Event. With most of the venues for the Paris Games now confirmed, the teams that did not qualify in the Bahamas will have another opportunity to secure their spots based on the world rankings.

title: SA Men's 4x100m relay team qualifies for Paris Olympics
author: Luke Menezies
description: South Africa's men's relay teams shine at World Relay Event, securing silver and qualifying for Paris Olympics in stunning performances.
